completedItems: Added deterministic `OrganizedUserMessage` and `OrganizedUserMessageGroup` scaffolding in `app/src/convex/chatContext.ts` for consecutive user-message runs, including ordered ids, one-based positions, raw text, bounded excerpts, and `shouldAddressIndividually`.
Refactored consecutive-user detection to sort by timestamp and preserve chronological order while keeping the existing bounded `contextWindowConfig.consecutiveUserLimit`.

Updated placeholder assistant completion in `app/src/convex/chat.ts` to use the organized group scaffold and produce replies that explicitly frame multiple consecutive user messages as separate threads to answer one by one.

Preserved underlying persisted message records and chronological history: no schema changes or UI chronology changes were introduced; the existing `groupedMessageIds` metadata remains the bridge from pending assistant replies to source user messages.
Added Node test coverage in `app/src/convex/chatContext.test.ts` for organized group ids, positions, excerpts, and individual-addressing flag.
Committed changes as `026e33e feat(chat): organize consecutive user message replies`.
unfinishedItems: Manual UX validation of how the organized placeholder text reads in the live chat remains for validation/user testing.
No changes were made to the unrelated modified `.DS_Store` file.

issuesDiscovered: The first type-check caught that Convex document filtering needed an explicit type predicate to narrow grouped messages to role `user`; fixed in this pass.
The first lint run caught formatting drift in the new test; fixed with Prettier.
Existing generated Convex lint warnings remain non-blocking: unused eslint-disable directives in `app/src/convex/_generated/*`.
Unrelated `.DS_Store` remains modified in the working tree.

validationAssertionsTouched.notes: Consecutive latest user messages are detected in timestamp order, bounded by `contextWindowConfig.consecutiveUserLimit`, and exposed as ids plus organized group items.
Grouped placeholder replies now list each user message as a separate numbered thread and state they will be answered one by one.
The implementation only adds deterministic context organization and reply text generation; it does not merge or reorder persisted messages.
The organized group types provide deterministic message ids, positions, text, and excerpts suitable for future small-model/grouped-agent interfaces.
Added test coverage for organized consecutive user-message grouping behavior.
